title: Multi-tab assistant implementation checklist
desc: Execution checklist for implementing a multi-tab assistant interface (Claude, Codex, Copilot) in phases, scoped to extension-level interface and orchestration behavior.
steps: Freeze scope before coding: interface and orchestration parity only, no private backend reverse engineering.
Create provider abstraction first: define IProviderAdapter with stream, cancel, retry, health-check, and usage-estimate methods.
Implement canonical event bus: user_message, assistant_delta, assistant_done, tool_call, tool_result, error, and status events.
Build tab/session state store keyed by workspace + provider + thread id; enforce isolation across tabs.
Implement streaming renderer with deterministic buffering and cursor-safe partial updates.
Add stop/retry/regenerate controls and map them to adapter cancel/retry semantics per provider.
Add explicit context panel for attached files, active selection, cwd, and recent commands with per-item remove toggles.
Implement permission gateway for tool actions: allow once, allow for session, deny, and deny-all policies.
Implement resilient error surfaces: provider timeout, auth failure, rate limit, offline mode, and degraded fallback notice.
Add budget controls: token estimate preflight, soft warning threshold, hard-stop threshold, and per-session usage summary.
Implement persistence and resume: write compact conversation snapshots, restore tabs on restart, and recover in-flight status safely.
Add integration tests per phase: stream ordering, tab isolation, cancellation race, permission denial path, and restore correctness.
Ship in four milestones with acceptance gates: M1 skeleton, M2 adapters, M3 permissions and persistence, M4 polish and observability.
author: claude
